things.name: Strathclyde Country Park
things.description: Scotland's busiest country park with a water sports loch, theme park, Roman bathhouse and year-round events

things.name: Baron's Haugh RSPB Nature Reserve
things.description: Mosaic of marsh, woodland and meadow beside the River Clyde — an oasis for kingfishers, otters and wintering wildfowl

things.name: Motherwell Heritage Centre
things.description: Free museum telling the remarkable story of Motherwell's steel and coal heritage, from ironworks to the present day

things.name: Dalzell Estate & Country Park
things.description: Scenic estate with woodland walks, a walled garden and the striking baronial Dalzell House overlooking the River Clyde

things.name: Bothwell Castle
things.description: Finest surviving example of 13th-century Scottish castle architecture — a massive sandstone fortress above the River Clyde

things.name: Chatelherault Country Park
things.description: Duke of Hamilton's grand 18th-century hunting lodge by William Adam set in a country park above the Avon gorge, with white Cadzow cattle

things.name: New Lanark World Heritage Site (Day Trip)
things.description: UNESCO-listed 18th-century cotton mill village founded by Robert Owen — a visionary community with a working mill and excellent visitor centre

things.name: David Livingstone Centre (Blantyre)
things.description: Birthplace museum of the Victorian explorer and missionary David Livingstone — charting his epic journeys across Africa

things.name: Fir Park Stadium (Motherwell FC)
things.description: Home of Motherwell FC — one of Scotland's oldest football clubs, founded 1886, with stadium tours available

things.name: Glasgow (Day Trip)
things.description: Scotland's largest city just 20 minutes by train — world-class museums, the Burrell Collection and a superb food and drink scene
